description: Fresh post-audit migration PRD for agentOS. This backlog supersedes the prior Ralph plan and reopens any work that is still incomplete, regressed, skipped, or only partially verified. The goal is to finish the Rust/kernel migration, remove remaining host-Node guest execution paths, restore agent and command-package parity, and make the full first-party test surface truthful and green.
testPolicy: Treat the old April 7-8, 2026 Ralph progress claims as stale. Use only the exact scoped commands listed in each story. US-001 and US-002 restore reproducible workspace install and normal pnpm/Vitest execution; no TypeScript-package story should be marked complete before those land. The final story requires root `pnpm test`, targeted crate suites (`agent-os-kernel`, `agent-os-bridge`, `agent-os-execution`, `agent-os-v8-runtime`, `agent-os-sidecar`), and the package/registry suites to pass with no first-party ignored Rust tests and no product-debt skips remaining.

userStories.title: Fix VFS layer visibility: vm.writeFile() must be visible to WASM guest processes (sh, ls, cat)
userStories.description: vm.writeFile() writes to a VFS layer that V8/Node guest processes can see but WASM processes cannot. Files written via the host API are invisible to sh, ls, cat running inside the VM.
acceptanceCriteria: vm.writeFile('/tmp/test.txt', 'hello') followed by vm.exec('cat /tmp/test.txt') returns 'hello'
vm.writeFile('/tmp/test.txt', 'hello') followed by vm.exec('ls /tmp/') lists test.txt
Files written via host API are on the same VFS layer as files visible to WASM processes
Test added to packages/core/tests/ verifying cross-layer visibility
pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run tests/filesystem.test.ts passes
userStories.notes: Reproduced: vm.writeFile writes to kernel upper VFS layer. Node guest processes see it (they use the kernel VFS bridge). But WASM processes (sh, ls, cat) apparently read from a different overlay or shadow directory that doesn't include the host-written upper layer. The fix likely needs WASM process mounts to include the same upper layer that the host API writes to.

userStories.title: Fix VFS layer visibility: agent session writes (Claude bash tool) must be visible to vm.readFile()
userStories.description: Files written by agent tools (via WASM sh/cat inside a session) are invisible to vm.readFile(). The agent confirms the file was written, exec cat sees it, ls lists it, but the host readFile API returns ENOENT.
acceptanceCriteria: After Claude agent writes /tmp/file.txt via bash tool, vm.readFile('/tmp/file.txt') returns the content
After vm.exec('echo x > /tmp/file.txt'), vm.readFile('/tmp/file.txt') returns 'x' (this already works)
After agent session writes, host API sees the same filesystem state as WASM processes
Test: quickstart agent-session.ts completes without ENOENT on readFile after agent write
pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run tests/filesystem.test.ts passes
userStories.notes: Reproduced: Claude bash tool writes via WASM sh → cat > file. exec('cat file') sees it, exec('ls') lists it, but vm.readFile() returns ENOENT. This is the REVERSE of US-CC-009 — the agent session's WASM child process writes to a shadow/overlay that the host readFile API doesn't query. The host API may only look at the kernel upper layer while WASM processes write to a separate shadow directory on the host filesystem. The fix needs vm.readFile() to check both the kernel upper layer AND any shadow directories where WASM processes wrote.

userStories.title: Fix @rivet-dev/agent-os workspace name → @rivet-dev/agent-os-core across all packages
userStories.description: The core package was renamed from @rivet-dev/agent-os to @rivet-dev/agent-os-core during migration but many package.json deps and TS imports still reference the old name, breaking pnpm install.
acceptanceCriteria: All package.json files reference @rivet-dev/agent-os-core (not @rivet-dev/agent-os) in dependencies
All TypeScript imports use @rivet-dev/agent-os-core
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile passes from repo root
pnpm build passes (all 46 workspace packages)
userStories.notes: BLOCKER for everything. The rename broke the workspace. Files already partially fixed in working tree but need lockfile regeneration. Check: package.json, all src/*.ts imports in packages/, registry/, examples/.

userStories.title: Fix node:events module shape — EventEmitter must be the default export
userStories.description: require('events') and require('node:events') must return the EventEmitter constructor function itself (not an object with EventEmitter as a property). Claude SDK does `class X extends require('events')` which breaks with wrong shape.
acceptanceCriteria: require('events') returns EventEmitter constructor (typeof === 'function')
require('events').EventEmitter === require('events') (same reference)
class MyEmitter extends require('events') {} works without error
new (require('events'))() creates a working EventEmitter
Test in crates/sidecar/tests/builtin_conformance.rs verifies this
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar --test builtin_conformance -- --test-threads=1 passes
userStories.notes: Found in step-23 codex session. Claude SDK and many npm packages use `extends require('events')`. The v8-bridge.js events module from node-stdlib-browser returns the constructor correctly but the bridge wrapper may be wrapping it in an object. Check v8-bridge.source.js events import and how it's exposed to guest code.

userStories.title: Fix stream async iterator — Symbol.asyncIterator for Readable streams
userStories.description: Readable streams must support `for await (const chunk of stream)` via Symbol.asyncIterator. Claude SDK uses this for streaming API responses.
acceptanceCriteria: Readable streams from require('stream') have Symbol.asyncIterator method
for await (const chunk of readableStream) yields chunks correctly
Anthropic SDK response streaming works: for await (const event of stream) {...}
Test verifies async iteration on a Readable stream
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 passes
userStories.notes: Found in step-23. Claude SDK uses `for await (const event of response.stream)`. node-stdlib-browser's readable-stream package should have this, but it may need explicit wiring in the v8-bridge. Check if readable-stream v3+ is bundled (it has native async iterator support).

userStories.title: Fix WASM child process stdout relay — proc_spawn/waitpid deadlock
userStories.description: WASM processes that spawn children (sh, xu) deadlock because the synchronous RPC model blocks the WASM thread while waiting for child stdout. Replace sync poll loop with async event dispatch.
acceptanceCriteria: vm.exec('sh -c "echo hello"') returns 'hello' without hanging
vm.exec('xu hello-agent-os') returns expected output without hanging
WASM sh can spawn and wait for child processes (node scripts, other WASM commands)
Child stdout/stderr flows back to parent WASM process correctly
No deadlock when parent polls for child exit while child has pending stdout
Test: cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 wasm passes
Test: vitest run tests/process-management.test.ts passes
userStories.notes: ROOT CAUSE: WASM calls proc_waitpid → callSyncRpc('child_process.poll') → blocks in readSync(). Child stdout needs to flow through WASM fd_read → which calls callSyncRpc AGAIN → DEADLOCK (single-threaded WASM). Secure-exec solved this with async event dispatch via _childProcessDispatch callback. The sidecar should PUSH child events to WASM guest via StreamCallback instead of waiting for guest to poll. See .agent/recovery/secure-exec/nodejs/src/bridge/child-process.ts for the working pattern. Step-26 codex made partial fixes (synthetic pipe bookkeeping, stdin EOF, FD lifetime) but the core deadlock remains.

CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_DEV_NULL_REDIRECT=1 set

userStories.title: Quickstart agent-session.ts works E2E with Claude without modification
userStories.description: The examples/quickstart/src/agent-session.ts file runs successfully with Claude agent, connecting to real Anthropic API, executing a prompt, and returning a response.
acceptanceCriteria: cd examples/quickstart && source ~/misc/env.txt && npx tsx src/agent-session.ts completes without error
Session ID is printed
Events are streamed (at least one session/update event)
Response text is printed with a meaningful answer
Session closes cleanly, process exits with code 0
No modifications to agent-session.ts needed (works as checked in)
Total runtime under 60 seconds
userStories.notes: INTEGRATION TEST. This exercises the full stack: workspace install → build → AgentOs.create() → sidecar spawn → V8 runtime → Claude adapter → Anthropic API → tool execution → response. When we tried this, it got Session ID printed but then timed out waiting for sidecar protocol frame for session_request. The V8 runtime binary was missing (needed cargo build -p agent-os-v8-runtime). After building, it still timed out — likely the Claude adapter boot issue (US-CC-005/006). This story should pass after all CC prerequisites are fixed.

userStories.description: As a developer, I want one terminal verification story that proves the repo is in a clean, fully tested state so the backlog can close on an auditable end condition instead of partial green claims.
acceptanceCriteria: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `pnpm check-types`, and `pnpm test` pass from the repo root in a clean workspace state
